header_summary:
Recent discussions across various platforms have elucidated significant technical and strategic considerations in blockchain and cryptocurrency developments.

1. A significant challenge in blockchain environments involves the potential for miners to initiate a rollback by mining a "revisionist" chain, abandoning the "authentic" chain. This strategy entails substantial risks, including the negation of previously mined earnings and potential ostracization by exchanges, due to its resemblance to a massive double-spend attack. Such actions could depreciate the value of cryptocurrencies and disrupt financial transactions, highlighting a need for preventive measures like an EC disabling fork to protect network integrity [more details](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/Mh9z4ISxh4iA80hltDJ3_u7ZWSO5pFuMYI9ae0aGIoSXJDdTOi2KiI4Ckm9BfUAY7YEMvTofep7hyKsA7Qjk6ydqk2GqG9fvOzEym9YQKjc=@proton.me/T/#mfab3a03d0d786b69c0e40b0542e22d7ddba7b3d6).

2. In the context of quantum computing's impact on cryptocurrencies, discussions have focused on integrating Post-Quantum (PQ) cryptographic schemes into Bitcoin Improvement Proposals (BIPs). Fabian's analysis emphasizes the need for new witness versions and output types, considering the data costs and public key visibility. The strategic preparation for Cryptographically Relevant Quantum Computers (CRQCs) involves consensus on PQ schemes' timeline and impacts, suggesting a shift towards transaction consolidation and the use of CoinJoins [Fabian's insights](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/Uk5a8rhAaNaCC8sif05lv1rmZLRYBCedbNVxPwDvHsWAA9Gbm36vGOw4XFvh7XG_MAJtiYBaD6BSEIr_KYWIxS5Ccm0OhwEIZo2gFeFfXvs=@protonmail.com/T/#m59ff00c66339cb64374ca910fbbfb95abc531e58).

3. The operational dynamics between cryptocurrency miners and mining pools reveal critical insights into the efficiency and stability of mining operations. The proxy system used in these relationships, while beneficial for aggregate computation, fails to accurately reflect individual miner performance, especially during disruptions. This discrepancy highlights areas for improvement in monitoring and managing miner outputs to enhance overall operational efficiency [further discussion](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/research-a-clockless-vardiff-strands-a-slowing-miner/2718/5).

4. Security vulnerabilities within Core Lightning's architecture, specifically in the `connectd` and `gossipd` daemons, were identified and addressed in recent updates. These vulnerabilities, exploitable by remote peers, could cause unbounded memory growth and system crashes. The resolution involved enhancing daemon communications and implementing message handling cutoffs, emphasizing the importance of rigorous testing and cautious handling of external inputs in software systems [vulnerability details and fixes](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/vulnerability-disclosure-twin-memory-exhaustion-dos-vulnerabilities-in-core-lightning/2731).

recent_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
recent_posts.summary: - Mining a revisionist chain involves abandoning the authentic blockchain.  - Success in such mining acts as a massive double-spend, disrupting system integrity.  - Shallow reorgs threaten network stability; preemptive disabling forks could mitigate risks.

recent_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
recent_posts.summary:
- Fabian discusses integrating Post-Quantum cryptography into Bitcoin proposals.
- He suggests new technical structures for transactions but notes a need for new output types.
- Fabian assesses trade-offs in key visibility, data cost, and network consensus needs for PQ schemes.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
recent_posts.summary:
- The Bitcoin mailing list debates the effectiveness of UTXO rescue protocols post-Quantum Day.
- Concerns arise about incomplete coverage due to varied address types and usage histories.
- Discussions emphasize the importance of understanding Knowledge Asymmetries in rescue operations.

recent_posts.title: [Research] A clockless vardiff strands a slowing miner
recent_posts.summary:
- Cryptocurrency miners connect to pools through a proxy for direct interaction.
- Liveness checks every 30 seconds aim to isolate non-responsive miners.
- Pools struggle to accurately assess individual miner performance due to aggregation.

recent_posts.title: Vulnerability Disclosure: Twin Memory Exhaustion DoS Vulnerabilities in Core Lightning
recent_posts.summary:
- Two critical Denial-of-Service vulnerabilities were discovered in Core Lightning's `connectd` and `gossipd` daemons.
- The vulnerabilities, exploitable by remote peers, caused unbounded memory growth and system crashes.
- Fixes were implemented in releases v26.04 and v26.06rc2, enhancing network stability and efficiency.

recent_posts.title: [Research] Bitcoin After Block Rewards : preventing miner's deviation when the Bitcoin rewards is zero
recent_posts.summary: - The revision will note results depend on a non-reactive defender model.  - The paper focuses on a conservative analysis of worst-case scenario outcomes.  - The author will not extend research due to personal reasons, urging others to explore.

active_posts.title: Quantum Recovery Of Hashed Address Secured Coins With No Confiscatory Risk
active_posts.summary: - Quantum computing threatens cryptocurrency security, prompting new signature scheme adoption.  - Various methods, including hierarchical and stateful proofs, aim to secure crypto post-quantum.  - Non-hashed addresses remain vulnerable despite multiple recovery proposals in place.

active_posts.title: BIP draft: CISA for Taproot Key Path Spends
active_posts.summary:
- A new BIP draft proposes advancements in Bitcoin transaction signatures via CISA.
- It offers three signature aggregation options and maintains traditional script paths.
- Feedback is sought via draft text and a mock pull request available online.

active_posts.title: The game-theory problems of PQ sunsetting modes
active_posts.summary:
- The "tripwire" scenario introduces a puzzle that could freeze all legacy Bitcoin coins.  - Collusion between miners and quantum entities could exploit $215 billion in legacy P2Pk coins.
- Quantum advancements necessitate reevaluation of security measures for blockchain integrity.

active_posts.title: P2share: how to turn any network (or testnet!) into a bitcoin miner
active_posts.summary:
- The p2share network allocates mining rewards uniquely, awarding entire rewards to a single shareholder.
- This method maintains fairness and efficiency in Bitcoin mining by keeping coinbase outputs small.
- The network fosters a decentralized mining pool, influencing future blockchain and cryptocurrency innovations.

active_posts.title: Onion Message Jamming in the Lightning Network
active_posts.summary: - The Lightning Network's onion message routing is prone to jamming and abuse.  - Various Lightning Network implementations adopt unique rate-limiting systems to curb vulnerabilities.  - Proposed mitigation strategies aim to secure messaging while addressing spam and network disruption.

active_posts.title: A Bitcoin-native LLM: dataset, architecture and open questions
active_posts.summary:
- Enhancing general-purpose LLMs to specialize in Bitcoin-related tasks includes interpreting scripts and transaction patterns.
- The development requires a comprehensive dataset from Bitcoin Improvement Proposals and real-world scripts.
- The architecture mixes a fine-tuned base model and a live-data tool-calling layer, promoting utility in varied scenarios.

today_in_history_posts.title: The Cryptographic Relay: An Electrical Device For Smart Transferable Hardware
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- A Cryptographic Relay allows control of electrical circuits via a public-private key system.
- Succinct Atomic Swaps enable asset exchange but are incompatible with the Lightning Network.
- Cryptographic Relays can validate ownership changes using a time-proof header chain.

today_in_history_posts.title: Dynamic Commitments: Upgrading Channels Without On-Chain Transactions
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Rusty proposes adding `commit_switch` and `commit_switch_reply` to Lightning Labs.  - He suggests maintaining an initiator-only system to manage message collisions.  - Upgrading to anchor_outputs will occur in three systematic steps.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- Passive investment favors minimal involvement, while active investment demands direct management.
- Potential pitfalls of passive investment include misaligned interests and underutilized assets.
- The blog discusses leveraging Bitcoin to stabilize economies through controlled fiscal strategies.
